description: Locale conventions for the Philippine market. Configures date formatting (DD/MM/YYYY), currency display (PHP — Philippine Peso), number formatting (period decimal, comma thousands), and address formatting patterns appropriate for Philippine healthcare facilities.

readiness.evidenceSummary: Pack manifest exists (locale-ph.json) defining PH locale conventions. Configuration keys for dateFormat, currencyCode, and numberFormat are declared. No runtime locale configuration or formatting infrastructure exists yet.
readiness.scopeBounds: en-PH locale only. Covers date, currency, number, and address formatting for Philippine market.
constrainingFactors: locale-ph pack is draft — no runtime locale configuration exists.
No formatting infrastructure implemented in platform or distro.
Locale switching and multi-locale tenant support not yet designed.

packDependencies.rationale: PH locale conventions pack provides all formatting configuration for the Philippine market.

claimPosture.permittedScopeStatement: Philippine locale pack manifest has been authored as a planning artifact defining PH-specific formatting conventions. No runtime locale implementation exists yet.
prohibitedClaims: Must not claim 'PH locale support' is implemented or available.
Must not claim date/currency/number formatting works for PH market.

visibilityPosture.rationale: Locale capability is visible in control-plane for PH market planning but not activatable. Hidden from tenant admins until formatting infrastructure exists.

activationConstraints: locale-ph pack must reach at least published lifecycle state.
Runtime formatting infrastructure must exist in platform.
No locale formatting infrastructure exists yet.

notes: PH-market-specific capability. Other markets require their own locale packs and capability instances.
en-PH locale uses English text with Philippine formatting conventions.
Future fil-PH locale would require separate locale pack if Filipino-language formatting differs.
